channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: 3 storylines to watch as Luka Doncic, Lakers return from the All-Star break
item.description: From incorporating their new star to seeing if they can sustain success with their small-ball lineups, the Lakers have 30 regular-season games to try to improve their playoff position.
item.dc:creator: Khobi Price, The Mercury News

item.title: Researchers confirm Hall of Famer Bobby Hull had CTE when he died 2 years ago
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Bobby Hull, the Hall of Fame forward and two-time NHL MVP who helped the Chicago Blackhawks win the Stanley Cup in 1961, had chronic traumatic encephalopathy when he died two years ago, his widow said in a statement released on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Tennis participation grows to more than 25M players in the US even as pickleball's popularity surges
item.description: Tennis participation in the United States rose to 25.7 million players in 2024, the sport's fifth consecutive year of growth, the U.S. Tennis Association said Wednesday, citing an analysis of numbers drawn from two studies not yet fully released.
item.dc:creator: HOWARD FENDRICH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: WNBA star Brittney Griner pulls out of speaking engagement after finding concerning message near hotel room
item.description: The Atlanta Dream center's cancelation stemmed from growing concerns related to security, organizers of the cannabis leadership convention said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baltimore Ravens' Ben Cleveland is arrested on a charge of drunken driving in Georgia
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — National Football League player Ben Cleveland of the Baltimore Ravens has been charged with drunken driving in Georgia, according to a sheriff's report obtained by The Associated Press on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Right-hander Nick Pivetta, Padres finalize $55M four-year contract
item.description: Right-hander Nick Pivetta and the San Diego Padres officially announced a finalized $55 million, four-year contract on Monday, a backloaded deal that allows him to opt out after the 2026 and '27 seasons.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500 for Hendrick Motorsports
item.description: DAYTONA BEACH, Fla (AP) — William Byron fortuitously missed the ferocious wrecks down the stretch at the Daytona 500 that knocked out contenders racing for the checkered flag and left him with a repeat victory in sight.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Aggies lose heartbreaker at first place New Mexico
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE (ABC4 Sports) - With first place in the Mountain West men’s basketball standings on the line, Utah State (22-4, 12-3 MW) fell just short on Sunday in Albuquerque, dropping an 82-79 thriller at The Pit. This marked the second time in two weeks the Aggies have lost to the Lobos.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Trump takes laps on Daytona 500 speedway in The Beast
item.description: “This is your favorite president,” Trump said. “I’m a big fan. I’m a really big fan of you people. How you do this, I don’t know. But I just want you to be safe.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Boys Basketball: Chula Vista 58, Sweetwater 56
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys basketball Chula Vista at Sweetwater with the Spartans edging out the Red Devils 58-56. Both teams now 4-1 in league play with the season winding down and league title still up for grabs.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Fraser Bullock, Brad Wilson, Steve Starks to lead organizing committee for 2034 Olympic Games
item.description: State and Olympic officials named a 25-person organizing committee, led by Committee President Fraser Bullock, former House Speaker Brad Wilson, and Utah business executive Steve Starks.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4

item.title: Philadelphia Eagles Super Bowl parade
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — On this Feb. 14, love Hurts. At least in Philadelphia, where swooning Eagles fans are ready to celebrate the Super Bowl champions and MVP quarterback Jalen Hurts across the City of Brotherly Love.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A new professional soccer team might be coming to this Southern California city
item.description: A new stadium and entertainment district fit for a professional soccer team might be coming to Los Angeles County.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Jazz turn the tables on Lakers in 131-119 victory
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - What a difference 48 hours make. On Monday, the L.A. Lakers blew away the Utah Jazz in Luka Doncic' Laker debut. But Wednesday in Salt Lake City, it was all Jazz.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Holmoe will be a part of search for his replacement
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After 20 years on the job, Tom Holmoe decided this past summer that this would be his last year as BYU's athletic director. "I made the decision in July of this year," Holmoe said at his retirement press conference Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
